141: Andrew Carnegie was a Scottish-American industrialist and philanthropist. He had a significant impact on Pittsburgh, accumulating wealth and building the Carnegie Steel Company, which he sold to J.P. Morgan in 1901. After selling the company, he devoted his life to large-scale philanthropy, funding local libraries, working for world peace, education, and scientific research.
